response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and extensive experience with complex systems, they lack the specific technical expertise required for this role. There is no evidence of Spring ecosystem experience (explicitly required), minimal recent Java/Kotlin work, no Kotlin mentioned at all, and IDE tooling experience is limited to 2000-2001. The candidate's primary focus over the past 13+ years has been on web/backend systems using TypeScript and Node.js, not JVM enterprise development, creating a significant gap between their expertise and the role's requirements.
strengths: Extensive track record building complex, large-scale systems (microservices handling millions of requests/second, distributed GIS platforms, real-time data integration)
Strong commitment to software craftsmanship with proven expertise in TDD, CI/CD, and clean architecture principles across multiple projects
Demonstrated ability to learn and master new programming languages and domains, with experience spanning 7+ languages and diverse industries
concerns: No visible Spring ecosystem or enterprise framework experience despite this being an explicit requirement; no Kotlin mentioned in skills or projects
Current role (2011-present) focuses on TypeScript, Node.js, and AI infrastructure; no recent JVM enterprise development after 13+ year gap since last Java-focused work
IDE internals experience limited to 2000-2001 at National Instruments; subsequent career has focused on web/backend systems rather than developer tools or language tooling

key_factors.factor: Recent JVM and deep Java/Kotlin focus
Complex systems and software craftsmanship experience
